Comment: Healthcare Metrics ingestion: GDrive -> S3 (Bronze) -> Redshift Silver, for both PBJ Daily Nurse Staffing and NH Provider Info monthly snapshots. Each Drive file is classified by filename (RouteByFileType) into its own copy/merge-or-load branch. SQL is bound via Parameters (not string-interpolated) to avoid injecting unsanitized S3 keys into generated SQL. A pre-check (CheckPbjDuplicates / CheckNhDuplicates) rejects any file with duplicate key rows straight to the DLQ before its merge/load proc is ever called. Every terminal path (success or DLQ) writes merge_status back to the DynamoDB control table so a failure after the Lambda has already recorded the file's md5 doesn't get silently skipped on the next run. The merge/load retry is bounded to one extra attempt before falling through to the DLQ.

ReportLambdaErrorsToDLQ.Comment: Files that failed inside the Lambda itself (bad filename classification, Drive download failure, etc.) land in $.ingestion.errors, never $.ingestion.processed, so they'd otherwise never reach the Map below and would silently vanish with no DLQ trace. Runs over an empty array (the common case) as a zero-iteration no-op.

CheckPbjDuplicates.Comment: Pre-check before ever calling sp_merge_pbj_staffing: drive_modified_at/md5hash are constant for every row in a single load (one file per call), so a repeated provnum+workdate here is by construction 'identical' on every lineage dimension we have — it means the file is malformed, not that a winner needs picking. Returns rows only when a duplicate exists.

Parameters.Sql: SELECT provnum, workdate, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM staging.pbj_daily_nurse_staffing_stg WHERE provnum IS NOT NULL AND workdate IS NOT NULL GROUP BY provnum, workdate HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

CheckNhDuplicates.Comment: Pre-check before ever calling sp_load_nh_provider_info: a repeated provnum within one file has no distinguishing lineage (drive_modified_at/md5hash are constant for every row in a single load), so a repeat means the file is malformed, not that a winner needs picking. Returns rows only when a duplicate exists.

Parameters.Sql: SELECT provnum, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM staging.nh_provider_info_stg WHERE provnum IS NOT NULL GROUP BY provnum HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

PublishSilverLoadSucceeded.Comment: Fires once per state-machine execution, after every file in this run has been processed (successfully or via its own DLQ route) -- not once per file. This is what triggers the gold refresh state machine: an EventBridge rule matches on source/detail-type below and starts that execution directly, so gold always runs because silver just finished, not on a timer guessing whether it did. Fires regardless of per-file outcomes inside the Map -- individual file failures are already handled there, and gold rebuilding from whatever silver currently has is correct either way.

SendGoldTriggerErrorToDLQ.Comment: Silver already loaded successfully at this point -- a failed publish only means gold won't be triggered for this run, not that ingestion itself failed. Traced here (rather than silently continuing to IngestionComplete) so a missed gold trigger is visible instead of silently skipped; the next successful ingestion run's publish will trigger a gold refresh that rebuilds from everything currently in silver anyway, so this self-heals.
